This market will resolve to “Yes” if Charles Leclerc wins the 2024 Qatar Grand Prix scheduled for December 1, 2024. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

If the 2024 Qatar Grand Prix is canceled or rescheduled to a date after December 15, 2024, this market will resolve to “No.”

The resolution source will be the official Formula 1 website and credible sports news reporting.

title: Will Sergio Perez leave Red Bull?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if it is announced that Sergio "Checo" Perez will leave Red Bull Racing for the 2025 F1 Season by December 31, 2024 at 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Note: If it is announced that Perez will leave Red Bull Racing, reguardless of if his next team getting announced, this market will resolve to "Yes".

The resolution source will be official statements from Red Bull Racing and Sergio Pérez and his official representatives, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

title: F1 Constructors Champion
description:
This market will resolve according to the Constructor’s Championship for the 2025 F1 season. 

In the case of a tie between two teams, this market will resolve in favor of the team whose listed name comes first alphabetically.

As soon as the results for the final scheduled race of the 2025 F1 season are known this market will resolve.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2025 F1 Constructor's Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), this market will resolve to “No.”

If the F1 season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by February 28, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be information from F1.

title: F1 Spanish Grand Prix: Which Constructor scores the most points?
description:
This market will resolve to the constructor team that collects the most points at the F1 Spanish Grand Prix, currently scheduled for June 1, 2025.

In the case of a tie between two teams, this market will resolve in favor of the team whose listed name comes first alphabetically.

As soon as the results for this event are known, this market will resolve.

title: F1 Spanish Grand Prix: Head to Head Matchups
description: This is a market on the head to head matchup results at the 2025 F1 Spanish Grand Prix, scheduled for June 1, 2025.

markets.question: Leclerc vs. Russell
markets.description:
This market is based on whether Charles Leclerc or George Russell finishes ahead of the other at the F1 Spanish Grand Prix, scheduled for June 1, 2025.

If Charles Leclerc finishes ahead of George Russell, this market will resolve to "Leclerc".
If George Russell finishes ahead of Charles Leclerc, this market will resolve to "Russell". 

In the case of a tie between two drivers, this market will resolve in favor of the driver whose listed name comes first alphabetically. 

The resolution source for this market will be information from F1.

markets.question: Alonso vs. Stroll
markets.description:

title: F1 Canadian Grand Prix: Safety Car?
description:
The F1 Canadian Grand Prix is scheduled for June 15, 2025. 

This market will resolve to "Yes" if a full Safety Car is deployed at any point during the race. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No". 

A Safety Car Period is defined as any point during the race where the Safety Car is deployed and leads the field by driving in front of the race leader. If the race begins under Safety Car conditions, the market will resolve to "Yes". If the race ends under Safety Car conditions but the Safety Car does not reach the front of the field before the finish, the market will still resolve to "Yes". 

Virtual Safety Car periods will not qualify for this market. 

If the 2025 F1 Canadian Grand Prix is canceled or postponed beyond July 15, 2025, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source for this market will be the official Formula 1 website and credible sports news reporting.
